ddls:
CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the model was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the model was last updated */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of model (e.g., regression, classification) */,
  "framework" TEXT /* Machine learning framework used */,
  "algorithm" TEXT /* Algorithm used for the model */,
  "hyperparameters" TEXT /* Hyperparameters of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was created */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the dataset (e.g., internal, third-party) */,
  "data_size" TEXT /* Size of the dataset */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the dataset (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "evaluations" (
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each evaluation record */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being evaluated */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used for evaluation */,
  "precision" REAL /* Precision of the model on the dataset */,
  "recall" REAL /* Recall of the model on the dataset */,
  "f1_score" REAL /* F1 score of the model on the dataset */,
  "false_positive" REAL /* Number of false positives */,
  "false_negative" REAL /* Number of false negatives */,
  "true_positive" REAL /* Number of true positives */,
  "true_negative" REAL /* Number of true negatives */,
  "tested_samples" INTEGER /* Number of samples tested */,
  "trained_samples" INTEGER /* Number of samples used for training */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date of the evaluation */,
  "metrics" TEXT /* Additional metrics (e.g., accuracy, loss) */,
  "evaluation_type" TEXT /* Type of evaluation (e.g., training, testing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evaluation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_evaluations_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_evaluations_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data scientist, ML engineer) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date the user last logged in */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the version record */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model associated with the version */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the model */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the version */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes about changes in this version */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Detailed list of changes in this version */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_comparisons" (
  "comparison_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comparison record */,
  "model_id_1" INTEGER /* ID of the first model being compared */,
  "model_id_2" INTEGER /* ID of the second model being compared */,
  "comparison_metrics" TEXT /* Metrics used for the comparison */,
  "comparison_date" TEXT /* Date of the comparison */,
  "results" TEXT /* Detailed results of the comparison */,
  "winner" TEXT /* ID of the winning model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comparison_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_comparisons_model_id_2 FOREIGN KEY ("model_id_2") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_comparisons_model_id_1 FOREIGN KEY ("model_id_1") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the evaluation */,
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the evaluation accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_evaluation_id FOREIGN KEY ("evaluation_id") REFERENCES evaluations ("evaluation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reporting" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model linked to the report */,
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the evaluation linked to the report */,
  "generated_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* Path to the generated report file */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reporting_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reporting_evaluation_id FOREIGN KEY ("evaluation_id") REFERENCES evaluations ("evaluation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing feedback */,
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the evaluation being commented on */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments from the user */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when feedback was provided */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (e.g., 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_evaluation_id FOREIGN KEY ("evaluation_id") REFERENCES evaluations ("evaluation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, in-app) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
